name: Zerodha Trading Service — GitHub Actions CI/CD
scenario: Zerodha's trading service is deployed manually 3 times per week, taking 20 minutes each time and occasionally deploying broken builds. Build a GitHub Actions pipeline that automates: test → build → push → deploy-staging → manual-approval → deploy-prod.

core.description: .github/workflows/cicd.yml: on push to main. Jobs: test (mvn test + maven cache + upload artifacts) → build-push (Docker + GHCR + SHA tag + layer cache) → deploy-staging (kubectl set image + rollout status). All with proper 'needs:' chain.
deploy-prod job: environment: production (manual approval gate). kubectl deploy. rollback-prod job: if: failure() on deploy-prod.
Integration tests job: postgres service container + Spring Boot integration tests with correct env vars.
@SpringBootTest (2 tests): health endpoint returns UP, custom counter increments. Both pass.
README: explains SHA tagging vs latest, and pipeline stages.

bonus.description: Trivy security scan job (after build, before deploy-staging). Fails pipeline on CRITICAL vulnerabilities.
Slack notification: on successful prod deploy + on prod failure (different messages with SHA, actor, workflow link).
Reusable deploy workflow: .github/workflows/deploy.yml with workflow_call trigger + inputs (image, env, namespace).

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. permissions: packages: write — required to push to ghcr.io (GitHub Container Registry). cache: maven in setup-java: automatically caches ~/.m2 between runs — saves 1-2 minutes per run. if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' on build-push job: prevents building/deploying on PR branches. if: failure() on rollback job: ONLY runs when the previous job (deploy-prod) fails. secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN: automatically available in every workflow, no setup needed; use for GHCR login.
